mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and stimulates credit expansion, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Below-median FEDFUNDS levels fail to coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor income and collapsing aggregate demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp within 3 months during any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in industrial production and retail sales confirms that manufacturing supply is expanding in tandem with consumer demand without inventory accumulation, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in INDPRO and RSAFS fail to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, accelerating consumer price inflation forces equity investors to price in additional federal funds rate hikes, raising discount rates and compressing valuation multiples, which causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases correspond with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of months over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling more than 10 percent below their trailing twelve-month average signal a contraction in residential construction activity and associated household wealth effects, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines exceeding 10% below the trailing 12-month average do not coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment combined with a rising federal funds rate indicates an overheating labor market vulnerable to monetary policy overshoot, where tighter financial conditions outweigh employment gains, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decreases month-over-month while FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: The combination of month-over-month falling UNRATE and rising FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month INDPRO changes fail to coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in retail sales signal a sustained deterioration in consumer spending that reduces corporate top-line growth, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Two consecutive monthly RSAFS declines do not correspond with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.
